Confession_Text: I have been staying in bed longer than usual. I do feel overwhelm sometimes when people have things to do than me. I don't know, I just felt okay by myself.
I am feeling great about my classes this semester. The lecturer for my major course is very engaging and friendly. I managed to finish all my lab reports early this week.

Lately I find it really hard to concentrate during long group project meetings. My chest feels tight whenever I look at the pile of assignments due next week. I try to stay positive but the fatigue is starting to catch up with me.

I am doing okay overall but I notice I get irritated much faster than usual over small things. Group assignments are moving a bit slow and it makes me anxious. I just need to get through this final presentation week cleanly.

Everything feels incredibly overwhelming right now and I cannot seem to stop crying in my room. My grades are dropping even though I spend every waking hour trying to study at the library. I feel completely disconnected from my friends and family.

I feel completely empty inside and I do not see the point of trying anymore. I have completely stopped attending my morning lectures because waking up feels like too much work. I just want to disappear and escape from all this academic pressure.

My semester is going quite smoothly and I am enjoying my elective subjects. I spent the evening catching up with my hostel mates at the cafeteria. I feel well-rested and prepared for my upcoming mid-semester quizzes.

It is becoming difficult to fall asleep at night because my mind keeps racing with negative thoughts. I feel like I am constantly lagging behind everyone else in my core engineering subjects. I smile and act happy around my classmates but inside I feel so lost.

I have been feeling unusually detached from my favorite hobbies lately. Usually, I love going for evening walks around the campus lake, but now I prefer staying isolated in my room. It feels like a minor cloud of sadness is hanging over me all day.

The stress from my final year design project is eating me alive every single day. I can barely eat anything because my stomach feels tied in knots from constant worry. I feel like a massive failure who is bound to let my entire group down.

I am genuinely happy with my academic progress this term. Our programming project team communicates exceptionally well and we completed our code baseline ahead of schedule. Waking up early for classes feels exciting and meaningful.

I find myself losing my temper constantly over trivial matters with my roommates. I am constantly exhausted but when I try to sleep, I just stare blankly at the ceiling for hours. I am terrified that I will lose my scholarship if my GPA drops.

I feel completely broken and helpless against this constant wave of deep exhaustion. No matter how much I sleep, I wake up feeling heavy, miserable, and entirely drained. I do not have the energy to reply to messages or talk to anyone at all.

Classes are somewhat demanding but completely manageable if I pace myself well. I went to the university gym yesterday to de-stress after a long day of data analytics testing. I feel steady and optimistic about the remaining weeks.

I feel a profound sense of sadness every morning when I open my eyes. I have started avoiding my regular friend group because I feel like an absolute burden to their conversations. Everything I try to do feels completely meaningless and dry.
